content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sun is ___ and it shines in the sky every day.
distractor_rationale: the sun is never purple — this would be nonsensical and contradicts common knowledge
black describes darkness or night, which is the opposite of a shining sun
a grey sun would mean cloudy or storm conditions, contradicting 'shines every day'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She drinks a glass of ___ milk every morning before school.
distractor_rationale: milk is not green — green milk does not exist and would be inedible
orange milk does not exist; orange juice is orange, not milk
pink milk does not exist as a natural drink — this contradicts the word 'milk'

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The night sky is ___ and you can see the stars clearly.
distractor_rationale: a white night sky would mean daytime or heavy snow clouds, contradicting 'see the stars'
a yellow sky occurs at sunrise or sunset, not at night when stars are visible
a blue sky is a daytime sky, which contradicts 'the night sky' stated in the sentence

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom picked a ___ apple from the tree and ate it for a snack.
distractor_rationale: grey apples do not exist — this creates a nonsensical description of a real fruit
purple apples do not exist as a common fruit variety, making this nonsensical
white apples do not exist as a recognised fruit colour — apples are red, green, or yellow

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The leaves on the trees turn ___ and fall to the ground every autumn.
distractor_rationale: leaves do not turn blue in autumn — this contradicts the natural process described
leaves do not turn pink when they fall in autumn — this is not a natural autumn colour
white leaves in autumn would suggest snow or frost on them, not the turning process described

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria wore a ___ dress to the party because it matched the colour of the sea.
distractor_rationale: orange does not match the colour of the sea — this contradicts the clue 'matched the colour of the sea'
yellow does not match the colour of the sea — this contradicts the stated reason for her choice
black does not match the colour of the sea — seawater is blue, not black

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She peeled an ___ fruit for breakfast and enjoyed its sweet, tangy juice.
distractor_rationale: a green fruit with sweet tangy juice is a lime, which is not typically peeled and drunk as juice at breakfast in the same way — and 'green' does not collocate with 'orange fruit'
grey fruit does not exist — this creates a nonsensical description
pink fruit does not match a fruit known for being peeled and having sweet tangy juice in this context

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor said the patient looked very ___ in the face, so she needed more time outdoors in the sun.
distractor_rationale: bright is not used to describe a person's unhealthy skin tone — it does not collocate with a face that needs sunlight
dark skin tone would not be a medical concern requiring more time in the sun — this contradicts the doctor's advice
an orange face would not prompt a doctor to recommend more sunlight — orange is not a colour associated with paleness or illness

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James has ___ hair, so people often think he is from a northern European country.
distractor_rationale: grey hair is associated with older people, not with a nationality stereotype about northern Europeans
dark hair is more commonly associated with southern European or other regions, which contradicts the northern European association in the sentence
red hair is associated with Celtic regions such as Ireland or Scotland, not specifically northern Europe as a whole

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The old man's hair turned completely ___ after he retired, and it was now the same colour as snow.
distractor_rationale: blonde hair does not match the colour of snow — snow is white or grey, not blonde
brown hair does not match the colour of snow — this contradicts the comparison to snow given in the sentence
red hair does not match the colour of snow — snow is not red, so this contradicts the stated comparison

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sun is ___ and very bright in the sky today.
distractor_rationale: blue describes the sky, not the sun itself, which is universally known to appear yellow
green is the colour of grass and plants, not the sun
pink is associated with sunsets or flowers, not the sun shining brightly in the daytime sky

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She poured milk into her coffee and it turned from ___ to light brown.
distractor_rationale: white is the colour of the milk being added, not the coffee before milk is poured in
red coffee does not exist as a standard drink; adding milk to red liquid would not produce light brown
orange coffee is not a recognised drink; adding milk to orange liquid would not produce light brown

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The traffic light changed to ___, so all the cars stopped immediately.
distractor_rationale: green means go at a traffic light, so cars would move forward, not stop
yellow means slow down or prepare to stop, not stop immediately
purple is not a traffic light colour and would make the sentence nonsensical

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom has ___ hair, just like his father — they both look very similar.
distractor_rationale: orange is not a natural human hair colour and would make the description unrealistic
grey hair is associated with older people, which contradicts the comparison to a father who looks similar to a young Tom
green is not a natural human hair colour and would make the sentence nonsensical in a realistic context

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sky outside became very ___ and cloudy just before the heavy rain started to fall.
distractor_rationale: bright skies are associated with sunny weather, which directly contradicts the heavy rain that follows
pink skies occur at sunrise or sunset in clear conditions, not before heavy rain
white skies suggest light cloud or fog, not the dark overcast conditions that immediately precede heavy rain

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Snow is ___ .
distractor_rationale: black is the opposite of white and contradicts the known colour of snow
red contradicts the universally known appearance of snow
green contradicts the universally known appearance of snow

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Bananas are ___ .
distractor_rationale: blue contradicts the universally known colour of ripe bananas
purple contradicts the universally known colour of ripe bananas
grey contradicts the universally known colour of ripe bananas

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sky is ___ on a sunny day.
distractor_rationale: orange describes the sky at sunset, not on a clear sunny day
pink describes the sky at dawn or dusk, not on a clear sunny day
brown contradicts the known appearance of a clear daytime sky

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria has ___ hair — it is very light, almost the colour of the sun.
distractor_rationale: dark describes hair that is not light, contradicting 'very light' in the sentence
black is the darkest hair colour, directly contradicting 'very light'
grey describes the colour of older or mixed hair, not hair described as 'almost the colour of the sun'

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom picked a ___ apple from the tree — it was not ripe yet and still very sour.
distractor_rationale: red apples are typically ripe and sweet, contradicting 'not ripe yet and still very sour'
yellow apples are generally ripe, contradicting 'not ripe yet and still very sour'
white is not a natural colour for apples on a tree

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The room was very ___ because all the curtains were closed and no light came in.
distractor_rationale: bright means full of light, which directly contradicts 'curtains closed and no light came in'
white describes a colour, not the absence of light in a room
orange describes a colour, not the absence of light in a room

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She painted the walls of her bedroom ___ because she wanted the room to feel warm and energetic.
distractor_rationale: grey is typically associated with a cool, neutral, or calm feeling, not warmth and energy
brown is associated with a dull or earthy feeling, not typically described as energetic
black is associated with darkness and heaviness, not a warm and energetic room feeling

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The nurse wore a ___ uniform, which is the traditional colour for hospital staff in many places.
distractor_rationale: purple is not a traditional or widely recognised colour for hospital uniforms
yellow is not a traditional or widely recognised colour for hospital uniforms
pink is not the traditional colour for hospital uniforms, though it may be used in some specific wards

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The old man had ___ hair and a long ___ beard, which made him look very wise.
distractor_rationale: blonde hair is associated with young or light-coloured hair, not typically with an old man looking wise
red hair is a specific natural colour not associated with ageing or wisdom in this context
bright is an adjective describing intensity of light or colour, not a specific hair colour

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna wore a ___ dress to the party because she wanted to look elegant, and the colour matched her black shoes perfectly.
distractor_rationale: brown does not typically collocate with 'elegant' party wear and does not create a strong visual contrast with black shoes
grey is a neutral colour not strongly associated with elegant party dresses in this context
dark is an adjective describing the depth of a colour, not a specific colour that can match black shoes

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sky is very ___ today. (BLUE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She has ___ hair, like the colour of the sun. (YELLOW)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The room looked ___ because there was very little light coming in. (DARK)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The walls of the old building had turned ___ after many years without paint. (GREY)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The leaves on the trees become ___ and orange every autumn before they fall. (RED)
